title: Buffer System Visualizer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactively adjust HA/A⁻ concentrations and pKa, then add strong acid/base to compare buffer vs. pure water pH changes.

learningObjectives: Use the Henderson-Hasselbalch equation to predict buffer pH.
Explain why buffers resist pH changes compared to pure water when strong acids/bases are added.

keyQuestions: How does changing [HA]/[A-] shift buffer pH?
Why does pure water exhibit larger ΔpH for the same acid/base addition?
commonMisconceptions: Adding equal moles of acid/base always neutralizes buffers completely.
assessmentOpportunities: Students log ΔpH for two additions and justify the difference.
Learners explain how choosing pKa near target pH improves buffering.
